# tmux Workstation Builder

> A single-page tool that turns a visual pane layout into a ready-to-paste `tmux` one-liner. No backend, no build step, no dependencies. The entire app is one HTML file. State is supplied via URL query parameters, so you (an agent) can compose a workspace as a link and hand it to the user.

Live URL: https://stawils.github.io/tmux-reference/
Source: https://github.com/stawils/tmux-reference

# How an agent uses it

Compose a URL with query parameters. Opening it pre-fills the builder and shows the generated `tmux` command in the Output panel (and auto-copied by the user). The same URL works for humans and agents.

## Query parameters

All optional. Any subset may be provided.

- `name`    — tmux session name. Default: `dev`. Example: `name=myproject`
- `path`    — working directory passed to `tmux -c`. Default: `~/workspaces/project`. Example: `path=~%2Fcode%2Fapp`
- `layout`  — pane arrangement. Accepts an index (0–7) or an alias (see below). Default: `side-by-side`.
- `detached` — `1`/`true`/`on` adds the `-d` flag (recommended; user attaches manually). `0` attaches immediately. Default: `1`.
- `panes`   — pipe-separated commands for each pane, in order. Example: `panes=nvim+.|cargo+run|lazygit`. Spaces may be `+` or `%20`. If omitted, panes default to `zsh`.
  - Alternative: pass individual panes as `pane0=...&pane1=...&pane2=...`.

## Layout aliases (for `layout=`)

- `0` / `single` / `one`        — 1 pane
- `1` / `side-by-side` / `2col` / `cols` / `horizontal` — 2 panes, left + right
- `2` / `stacked` / `2row` / `rows` / `vertical`       — 2 panes, top + bottom
- `3` / `main-side` / `main+side` / `main-vertical`    — wide main pane + narrow side
- `4` / `grid` / `2x2` / `quad`                        — 2×2 grid (4 panes)
- `5` / `main-3` / `main+3`                            — main pane + 3
- `6` / `3col` / `3columns`                            — 3 columns
- `7` / `custom` / `dynamic`                           — free-form (use any pane count)

## Example links

A 4-pane Rust dev workspace:
https://stawils.github.io/tmux-reference/?name=app&path=~%2Fworkspaces%2Fapp&layout=grid&detached=1&panes=nvim+.|cargo+run|lazygit|cargo+watch+-x+test

A simple editor + terminal split:
https://stawils.github.io/tmux-reference/?name=dev&layout=side-by-side&panes=nvim+.|zsh

Three SSH sessions side by side:
https://stawils.github.io/tmux-reference/?name=servers&layout=3col&panes=ssh+web1|ssh+web2|ssh+db1

# How to read the generated command

After a URL is opened, the generated `tmux` one-liner appears inside the element with `id="output"` (a `<pre>` tag). If you can execute JS (headless browser), load the URL and read `document.getElementById('output').textContent`. The text contains the command followed by a commented attach line:

```
tmux new-session -d -s app -c ~/workspaces/app \; split-window -h ... \; send-keys -t 0 'nvim .' Enter ...
# tmux attach -t app
```

# Command vocabulary (common values for `panes=`)

Editors: `nvim .`, `code .`, `hx .`, `vim .`
Test:    `cargo watch -x test`, `vitest`, `pytest -x`, `go test ./...`, `jest --watch`
Git:     `lazygit`, `tig`, `git log --oneline -20`, `git status`
Monitor: `htop`, `btop`, `journalctl -f`, `docker logs -f --tail 50`, `nethogs`
Run:     `cargo run`, `npm run dev`, `go run .`, `docker compose up`, `python main.py`, `make run`
Shell:   `zsh`, `bash`, `fish`, `clear`

# Other content on the page

The same page has two additional tabs with reference material (also useful to you):
- **Key Reference** — tmux keybindings (prefix is Ctrl+a)
- **Configuration** — copy-paste `~/.tmux.conf` snippets (prefix, general, mouse, Catppuccin status bar, vi copy mode, splits)
